# Claim Ledger Entry

## Identity

- Claim ID:
- Claim title:
- Claim owner:
- First registered:
- Last reviewed:
- Theory version:
- Status: proposed | active | supported-within-scope | weakened | falsified-within-scope | unresolved | superseded

## Exact Proposition

State one proposition only. It must be possible to specify what would count against it.

## Claim Type

- existence
- bounded sufficiency
- universality
- necessity
- minimality
- nontrivial constraint
- comparative economy
- replication
- implementation
- application

## Scope

State the independently justified reasoning-process class, time boundary, theory version, and exclusions.

## Assumptions

List every material assumption required for the claim.

## Dependencies

List theory artifacts, protocols, source reconstructions, implementations, verifiers, and adjudication rules on which the claim depends.

## Evidence For

| Evidence ID | Result | Evidential class | Independence class | Scope | Limitations |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| | | | | | |

## Evidence Against

| Evidence ID | Result | Evidential class | Scope | Implication |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| | | | | |

## Unresolved Evidence

| Evidence ID | Unresolved question | Missing information | Required next test |
|---|---|---|---|
| | | | |

## Strongest Counterexample

Describe the strongest known counterexample or pressure case, including why it has or has not been resolved.

## Strongest Competing Explanation

Describe the strongest alternative vocabulary, theory, or nonuniversal explanation currently available.

## Falsification Conditions

List observable results that would falsify the claim within its registered scope.

## Confidence-Raising Conditions

List evidence that would legitimately increase support.

## Confidence-Lowering Conditions

List evidence that would weaken support without necessarily falsifying the claim.

## Scope Changes

| Date | Previous scope | New scope | Trigger | Preregistered? | Effect on prior claim |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| | | | | | |

## Theory Revisions Triggered by Failure

| Version | Triggering evidence | Change | Original result retained at |
|---|---|---|---|
| | | | |

## Current Evidential Class

Select the highest justified class:

- internal coherence
- internal implementation robustness
- bounded sufficiency
- nontrivial constraint
- local necessity
- comparative economy
- independent technical replication
- adversarial conceptual replication
- generalized universality argument
- bounded or universal conclusion

## Current Judgment

State the strongest justified conclusion and no stronger conclusion.

## Nonclaims

Explicitly list attractive interpretations that the evidence does not establish.

## Next Decisive Test

Identify the smallest experiment most likely to change the current judgment rather than merely add another compatible example.
